insight: In the FIFA World Cup 2026, South Africa's performance during hydration breaks has been a mixed bag, with the team managing to gain momentum in only half of the breaks they've had, resulting in a slight decline in momentum at a rate of -0.050 per minute. This suggests that the breaks have not significantly boosted their performance, and instead, have led to a minor loss of steam. When South Africa wasn't pressing before the breaks, they had a similar decline in momentum, with an average change of -0.050 per minute, indicating that the breaks didn't provide the team with a significant advantage in terms of regaining their footing. Overall, the hydration breaks have had a neutral to slightly negative impact on South Africa's performance, failing to provide the team with a substantial boost in momentum.
